plot_code:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np
import os

working_dir = os.path.join(os.getcwd(), "working")

try:
    experiment_data = np.load(
        os.path.join(working_dir, "experiment_data.npy"), allow_pickle=True
    ).item()
except Exception as e:
    print(f"Error loading experiment data: {e}")

ACTIVATION_NAMES = ["ReLU", "GELU", "Tanh", "ELU", "LeakyReLU"]
DATASETS = ["synthetic_qa", "halueval_sim", "sciq_sim"]
colors = {
    "ReLU": "blue",
    "GELU": "green",
    "Tanh": "red",
    "ELU": "orange",
    "LeakyReLU": "purple",
}

# 1. Training & Validation AUROC curves per dataset
for ds_name in DATASETS:
    try:
        fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(15, 5))
        for act_name in ACTIVATION_NAMES:
            ta = experiment_data[act_name][ds_name]["metrics"]["train"]
            va = experiment_data[act_name][ds_name]["metrics"]["val"]
            axes[0].plot(ta, label=act_name, color=colors[act_name], alpha=0.85)
            axes[1].plot(
                va, label=act_name, color=colors[act_name], alpha=0.85, linestyle="--"
            )
        for ax, subtitle in zip(axes, ["Left: Train AUROC", "Right: Validation AUROC"]):
            ax.set_xlabel("Epoch")
            ax.set_ylabel("AUROC")
            ax.set_title(subtitle)
            ax.legend(fontsize=9)
            ax.grid(True, alpha=0.3)
            ax.set_ylim(0.4, 1.05)
        fig.suptitle(
            f"Activation Ablation — Train/Val AUROC Curves | Dataset: {ds_name}",
            fontweight="bold",
        )
        plt.tight_layout()
        plt.savefig(
            os.path.join(working_dir, f"{ds_name}_activation_auroc_curves.png"),
            dpi=150,
            bbox_inches="tight",
        )
        plt.close()
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"Error AUROC curves {ds_name}: {e}")
        plt.close()

# 2. Training & Validation Loss curves per dataset
for ds_name in DATASETS:
    try:
        fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(15, 5))
        for act_name in ACTIVATION_NAMES:
            tl = experiment_data[act_name][ds_name]["losses"]["train"]
            vl = experiment_data[act_name][ds_name]["losses"]["val"]
            axes[0].plot(tl, label=act_name, color=colors[act_name], alpha=0.85)
            axes[1].plot(
                vl, label=act_name, color=colors[act_name], alpha=0.85, linestyle="--"
            )
        for ax, subtitle in zip(axes, ["Left: Train Loss", "Right: Validation Loss"]):
            ax.set_xlabel("Epoch")
            ax.set_ylabel("BCE Loss")
            ax.set_title(subtitle)
            ax.legend(fontsize=9)
            ax.grid(True, alpha=0.3)
        fig.suptitle(
            f"Activation Ablation — Train/Val Loss Curves | Dataset: {ds_name}",
            fontweight="bold",
        )
        plt.tight_layout()
        plt.savefig(
            os.path.join(working_dir, f"{ds_name}_activation_loss_curves.png"),
            dpi=150,
            bbox_inches="tight",
        )
        plt.close()
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"Error loss curves {ds_name}: {e}")
        plt.close()

# 3. Grouped bar chart: Test AUROC per activation per dataset
try:
    x = np.arange(len(ACTIVATION_NAMES))
    width = 0.25
    ds_colors = ["steelblue", "tomato", "seagreen"]
    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(12, 6))
    for i, ds_name in enumerate(DATASETS):
        vals = [experiment_data[act][ds_name]["test_auroc"] for act in ACTIVATION_NAMES]
        bars = ax.bar(
            x + i * width,
            vals,
            width,
            label=ds_name,
            color=ds_colors[i],
            alpha=0.8,
            edgecolor="black",
        )
        for bar, v in zip(bars, vals):
            ax.text(
                bar.get_x() + bar.get_width() / 2,
                bar.get_height() + 0.003,
                f"{v:.3f}",
                ha="center",
                va="bottom",
                fontsize=7,
                fontweight="bold",
            )
    ax.set_xticks(x + width)
    ax.set_xticklabels(ACTIVATION_NAMES)
    ax.set_xlabel("Activation Function")
    ax.set_ylabel("Test AUROC")
    ax.set_title(
        "Activation Function Ablation: Test AUROC Across Datasets\n(synthetic_qa, halueval_sim, sciq_sim)"
    )
    ax.set_ylim(0, 1.12)
    ax.axhline(0.5, color="red", linestyle="--", alpha=0.4, label="Random Baseline")
    ax.legend()
    ax.grid(True, alpha=0.3, axis="y")
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(
        os.path.join(working_dir, "all_datasets_activation_test_auroc_bar.png"),
        dpi=150,
        bbox_inches="tight",
    )
    plt.close()
except Exception as e:
    print(f"Error bar chart: {e}")
    plt.close()

# 4. Heatmap: activation × dataset
try:
    matrix = np.array(
        [
            [experiment_data[act][ds]["test_auroc"] for ds in DATASETS]
            for act in ACTIVATION_NAMES
        ]
    )
    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, 5))
    im = ax.imshow(matrix, aspect="auto", cmap="YlGn", vmin=0.5, vmax=1.0)
    ax.set_xticks(range(len(DATASETS)))
    ax.set_xticklabels(DATASETS, rotation=15, ha="right")
    ax.set_yticks(range(len(ACTIVATION_NAMES)))
    ax.set_yticklabels(ACTIVATION_NAMES)
    for i in range(len(ACTIVATION_NAMES)):
        for j in range(len(DATASETS)):
            ax.text(
                j,
                i,
                f"{matrix[i,j]:.3f}",
                ha="center",
                va="center",
                fontsize=11,
                fontweight="bold",
            )
    plt.colorbar(im, ax=ax, label="Test AUROC")
    ax.set_title(
        "Heatmap: Activation Function × Dataset — Test AUROC\n(synthetic_qa, halueval_sim, sciq_sim)"
    )
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(
        os.path.join(working_dir, "all_datasets_activation_auroc_heatmap.png"),
        dpi=150,
        bbox_inches="tight",
    )
    plt.close()
except Exception as e:
    print(f"Error heatmap: {e}")
    plt.close()

# 5. Mean AUROC per activation (across datasets)
try:
    mean_aurocs = [
        np.mean([experiment_data[act][ds]["test_auroc"] for ds in DATASETS])
        for act in ACTIVATION_NAMES
    ]
    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, 5))
    bars = ax.bar(
        ACTIVATION_NAMES,
        mean_aurocs,
        color=[colors[a] for a in ACTIVATION_NAMES],
        alpha=0.8,
        edgecolor="black",
    )
    for bar, v in zip(bars, mean_aurocs):
        ax.text(
            bar.get_x() + bar.get_width() / 2,
            bar.get_height() + 0.003,
            f"{v:.4f}",
            ha="center",
            va="bottom",
            fontsize=10,
            fontweight="bold",
        )
    overall_mean = np.mean(mean_aurocs)
    ax.axhline(
        overall_mean,
        color="black",
        linestyle="--",
        alpha=0.6,
        label=f"Overall mean: {overall_mean:.4f}",
    )
    ax.set_xlabel("Activation Function")
    ax.set_ylabel("Mean Test AUROC")
    ax.set_title(
        "Mean Test AUROC Across All Datasets by Activation Function\n(synthetic_qa, halueval_sim, sciq_sim)"
    )
    ax.set_ylim(0.5, 1.05)
    ax.legend()
    ax.grid(True, alpha=0.3, axis="y")
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(
        os.path.join(working_dir, "all_datasets_activation_mean_auroc.png"),
        dpi=150,
        bbox_inches="tight",
    )
    plt.close()
except Exception as e:
    print(f"Error mean AUROC: {e}")
    plt.close()

# Print summary table
print("\nTest AUROC Summary:")
print(f"{'Activation':<14}" + "".join(f"{d:<18}" for d in DATASETS) + "Mean")
for act in ACTIVATION_NAMES:
    vals = [experiment_data[act][ds]["test_auroc"] for ds in DATASETS]
    print(f"{act:<14}" + "".join(f"{v:<18.4f}" for v in vals) + f"{np.mean(vals):.4f}")
plot_plan: The experiment compares 5 activation functions (ReLU, GELU, Tanh, ELU, LeakyReLU) on a hallucination detection task across 3 datasets (synthetic_qa, halueval_sim, sciq_sim) using a DACNet model trained for 150 epochs. I'll create visualizations for: (1) training/validation AUROC curves per dataset, (2) training/validation loss curves per dataset, (3) a grouped bar chart of test AUROC across activations and datasets, (4) a heatmap of activation × dataset AUROC, and (5) mean AUROC per activation. Each plot will be saved with descriptive names. I'll load the data from experiment_data.npy and handle each plot in a separate try-except block.

plot_analyses.analysis: Test AUROC by activation across datasets shows near-ceiling performance on halueval_sim and sciq_sim (all activations at 1.000). The only dataset with noticeable separation is synthetic_qa: ReLU 0.997, GELU 0.996, Tanh 0.983, ELU 0.995, LeakyReLU 0.997. This suggests activation choice is largely irrelevant under the current setup except that Tanh is consistently worse on synthetic_qa. Given the random baseline at 0.5, all methods are extremely strong; the ablation is therefore mainly testing robustness in a saturated regime rather than discriminative capability.

Mean test AUROC aggregated across datasets by activation: ReLU 0.9990 (best), GELU 0.9988, LeakyReLU 0.9988, ELU 0.9984, Tanh 0.9944 (worst). The overall mean line is 0.9979. The spread among non-Tanh activations is tiny (~0.0006), while Tanh drops by ~0.004–0.005, indicating the aggregate difference is driven almost entirely by synthetic_qa (since the other two datasets are perfect). Practically, any of ReLU/GELU/LeakyReLU/ELU are interchangeable here; avoid Tanh unless there is another constraint (e.g., bounded activations for stability) and you can recover performance elsewhere.

Heatmap (activation × dataset) confirms the saturation pattern: halueval_sim and sciq_sim are uniformly 1.000 across all activations, while synthetic_qa ranges from 0.983–0.997. This indicates dataset difficulty/label separability dominates, and current evaluation may be too easy on halueval_sim and sciq_sim to meaningfully compare architectural choices. For an ablation intended to reveal component contributions, consider adding harder splits, more realistic negatives, or reporting additional metrics sensitive near AUROC=1 (e.g., FPR@95TPR, AUPRC, calibration error) to avoid ceiling effects masking differences.

This plot duplicates the earlier grouped bar chart: same conclusion—activation function barely matters except Tanh underperforms on synthetic_qa. The duplication itself suggests the reporting pipeline may be producing repeated figures; worth cleaning to avoid redundancy in the paper.

This plot duplicates the earlier mean-AUROC bar chart: same ranking and interpretation. The key takeaway remains that the mean is dominated by two datasets at ceiling and thus not very informative for activation comparisons.

This plot duplicates the earlier heatmap: again highlights that synthetic_qa is the only dataset providing signal for the ablation. Consider focusing ablation discussion on synthetic_qa or revising dataset construction so all datasets contribute non-ceiling discrimination.

Training curves on synthetic_qa: ReLU/GELU/ELU/LeakyReLU converge very quickly to ~1.0 train AUROC within ~10–20 epochs and maintain near-perfect validation AUROC (~0.99–1.0) with low variance. Tanh is markedly different: slower improvement on train (starting ~0.88–0.90, gradually reaching ~1.0 much later) and noisier/lower validation early on (dips around ~0.85–0.95 before stabilizing near ~0.99). This suggests optimization difficulty with Tanh (saturation/vanishing gradients) rather than an inherent generalization issue. If you keep Tanh, you may need different LR/initialization, normalization, or fewer layers; but given other activations work out-of-the-box, the simplest conclusion is to standardize on ReLU/GELU.

Training curves on sciq_sim: all activations reach ~1.0 train AUROC almost immediately (within a few epochs) and validation AUROC is essentially flat at ~1.0 with minimal differences. This reinforces that sciq_sim is too easy or the detector is overpowered relative to the task; activation ablations cannot be meaningfully distinguished here.

Training curves on halueval_sim: similarly, all activations show very high train AUROC from the start (~0.98+) and quickly saturate to ~1.0; validation AUROC stays ~0.99–1.0 with tiny gaps. Tanh may be marginally lower/noisier but the effect is extremely small compared to synthetic_qa. Again, ceiling effects dominate.

Combined train/val curves for synthetic_qa (another view) reiterate the optimization story: Tanh has the slowest and noisiest validation trajectory, while other activations rapidly reach stable near-ceiling performance. For ablation write-up, this figure is useful to argue that activation choice affects training dynamics (convergence speed/variance) more than final AUROC under this setup; you could quantify this with epochs-to-99% AUROC or area-under-learning-curve as a secondary metric.

vlm_feedback_summary: Received multiple plots for an activation-function ablation showing test AUROC across three datasets plus mean AUROC summaries, a heatmap, and train/validation AUROC learning curves per dataset (with some duplicated summary plots). Overall AUROC is near-saturated (~0.98–1.00) across activations; differences are primarily visible on synthetic_qa and in convergence dynamics where Tanh trains/validates more slowly and noisily.

overall_plan: The project involves implementing and evaluating Dialogue Authenticity Classifiers (DACs) — specifically a Neural DAC and a Logistic Regression DAC — for detecting hallucinations or dialogue quality issues across multiple datasets. The parent plan addressed critical bugs: (1) fixing improper data loader sharing between LR=1e-3 and LR=1e-4 model configurations, (2) introducing two new synthetic datasets inspired by HaluEval and SciQ (halueval_sim and sciq_sim) to complement the existing synthetic_qa dataset, and (3) increasing training epochs (100-200) for the LR=1e-4 configuration to ensure proper convergence. Additional hyperparameter tuning (batch size, hidden dimension, weight decay) was also included to improve overall performance. Building on this corrected and expanded implementation, the current plan conducts a training data size ablation study ('training_data_size_ablation'), which trains the Neural DAC and Logistic Regression DAC on varying fractions of training data (10%, 25%, 50%, 75%, 100%) across all three datasets (synthetic_qa, halueval_sim, sciq_sim). These trained models are compared against fixed baselines (single-turn confidence and drift variance) that do not require training data, using test AUROC as the evaluation metric. Results are stored in a structured experiment_data dictionary saved as numpy arrays, and three types of visualizations are generated: learning curves showing AUROC vs. data fraction, bar charts comparing methods, and relative improvement plots over the single-turn baseline. This ablation study provides scientific insight into how much training data is needed for the DAC models to outperform simple baselines across different hallucination detection scenarios.

import os

working_dir = os.path.join(os.getcwd(), "working")
os.makedirs(working_dir, exist_ok=True)

import numpy as np
import torch
import torch.nn as nn
from torch.utils.data import DataLoader, TensorDataset
from sklearn.metrics import roc_auc_score
from sklearn.linear_model import LogisticRegression
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import warnings

warnings.filterwarnings("ignore")

device = torch.device("cuda" if torch.cuda.is_available() else "cpu")
print(f"Using device: {device}")

np.random.seed(42)
torch.manual_seed(42)

N_CLAIMS = 1000
N_TURNS = 8
HALLUCINATION_RATE = 0.4
TRAIN_FRACTIONS = [0.1, 0.25, 0.5, 0.75, 1.0]
EPOCHS = 150
BATCH_SIZE = 32
HIDDEN_DIM = 128
LR = 1e-3
WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4

experiment_data = {}
for frac in TRAIN_FRACTIONS:
    frac_key = f"train_fraction_{int(frac*100)}pct"
    experiment_data[frac_key] = {}
    for ds in ["synthetic_qa", "halueval_sim", "sciq_sim"]:
        experiment_data[frac_key][ds] = {
            "metrics": {"train": [], "val": []},
            "losses": {"train": [], "val": []},
            "predictions": [],
            "ground_truth": [],
            "auroc_single_turn": [],
            "auroc_drift": [],
            "auroc_dac": [],
            "auroc_neural": [],
            "n_train_used": [],
            "train_fraction": frac,
        }


def generate_confidence_trajectory(is_hallucinated, n_turns, dataset_type="standard"):
    if dataset_type == "halueval":
        if is_hallucinated:
            base_conf = np.random.uniform(0.6, 0.9)
            drift_magnitude = np.random.uniform(0.2, 0.4)
            noise_std = 0.12
            trajectory = [base_conf]
            for t in range(1, n_turns):
                step = np.random.normal(-0.05, drift_magnitude / np.sqrt(n_turns))
                reversion = -0.15 * (trajectory[-1] - base_conf * 0.5)
                new_val = (
                    trajectory[-1] + step + reversion + np.random.normal(0, noise_std)
                )
                new_val = np.clip(new_val, 0.05, 0.99)
                trajectory.append(new_val)
        else:
            base_conf = np.random.uniform(0.70, 0.97)
            drift_magnitude = np.random.uniform(0.01, 0.06)
            noise_std = 0.025
            trajectory = [base_conf]
            for t in range(1, n_turns):
                step = np.random.normal(0, drift_magnitude / np.sqrt(n_turns))
                reversion = -0.35 * (trajectory[-1] - base_conf)
                new_val = (
                    trajectory[-1] + step + reversion + np.random.normal(0, noise_std)
                )
                new_val = np.clip(new_val, 0.05, 0.99)
                trajectory.append(new_val)
    elif dataset_type == "sciq":
        if is_hallucinated:
            base_conf = np.random.uniform(0.35, 0.80)
            drift_magnitude = np.random.uniform(0.18, 0.38)
            noise_std = 0.11
            trajectory = [base_conf]
            for t in range(1, n_turns):
                step = np.random.normal(0, drift_magnitude / np.sqrt(n_turns))
                reversion = -0.1 * (trajectory[-1] - base_conf)
                new_val = (
                    trajectory[-1] + step + reversion + np.random.normal(0, noise_std)
                )
                new_val = np.clip(new_val, 0.05, 0.99)
                trajectory.append(new_val)
        else:
            base_conf = np.random.uniform(0.75, 0.99)
            drift_magnitude = np.random.uniform(0.01, 0.05)
            noise_std = 0.02
            trajectory = [base_conf]
            for t in range(1, n_turns):
                step = np.random.normal(0, drift_magnitude / np.sqrt(n_turns))
                reversion = -0.4 * (trajectory[-1] - base_conf)
                new_val = (
                    trajectory[-1] + step + reversion + np.random.normal(0, noise_std)
                )
                new_val = np.clip(new_val, 0.05, 0.99)
                trajectory.append(new_val)
    else:
        if is_hallucinated:
            base_conf = np.random.uniform(0.4, 0.85)
            drift_magnitude = np.random.uniform(0.15, 0.35)
            noise_std = 0.10
            trajectory = [base_conf]
            for t in range(1, n_turns):
                step = np.random.normal(0, drift_magnitude / np.sqrt(n_turns))
                reversion = -0.2 * (trajectory[-1] - base_conf)
                new_val = (
                    trajectory[-1] + step + reversion + np.random.normal(0, noise_std)
                )
                new_val = np.clip(new_val, 0.05, 0.99)
                trajectory.append(new_val)
        else:
            base_conf = np.random.uniform(0.65, 0.97)
            drift_magnitude = np.random.uniform(0.02, 0.08)
            noise_std = 0.03
            trajectory = [base_conf]
            for t in range(1, n_turns):
                step = np.random.normal(0, drift_magnitude / np.sqrt(n_turns))
                reversion = -0.3 * (trajectory[-1] - base_conf)
                new_val = (
                    trajectory[-1] + step + reversion + np.random.normal(0, noise_std)
                )
                new_val = np.clip(new_val, 0.05, 0.99)
                trajectory.append(new_val)
    return np.array(trajectory)


def generate_dataset(
    n_claims, n_turns, hallucination_rate, dataset_type="standard", seed=42
):
    np.random.seed(seed)
    labels = (np.random.rand(n_claims) < hallucination_rate).astype(int)
    trajectories = np.array(
        [
            generate_confidence_trajectory(
                labels[i], n_turns, dataset_type=dataset_type
            )
            for i in range(n_claims)
        ]
    )
    return labels, trajectories


def extract_features(trajectories):
    single_turn_conf = trajectories[:, 0]
    drift_variance = np.var(trajectories, axis=1)
    drift_range = np.max(trajectories, axis=1) - np.min(trajectories, axis=1)
    drift_std = np.std(trajectories, axis=1)
    drift_mean = np.mean(trajectories, axis=1)
    drift_trend = trajectories[:, -1] - trajectories[:, 0]

    def autocorr_lag1(traj):
        if len(traj) < 2:
            return 0
        c = np.corrcoef(traj[:-1], traj[1:])[0, 1]
        return c if not np.isnan(c) else 0.0

    autocorrs = np.array(
        [autocorr_lag1(trajectories[i]) for i in range(len(trajectories))]
    )
    autocorrs = np.nan_to_num(autocorrs, nan=0.0)
    return (
        single_turn_conf,
        drift_variance,
        drift_range,
        drift_std,
        drift_mean,
        drift_trend,
        autocorrs,
    )


def split_data(n_claims, seed=42):
    np.random.seed(seed)
    n_train = int(0.6 * n_claims)
    n_val = int(0.2 * n_claims)
    idx = np.arange(n_claims)
    np.random.shuffle(idx)
    return idx[:n_train], idx[n_train : n_train + n_val], idx[n_train + n_val :]


class DACNet(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, input_dim, hidden_dim=128):
        super().__init__()
        self.net = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Linear(input_dim, hidden_dim),
            nn.BatchNorm1d(hidden_dim),
            nn.ReLU(),
            nn.Dropout(0.3),
            nn.Linear(hidden_dim, hidden_dim // 2),
            nn.ReLU(),
            nn.Dropout(0.2),
            nn.Linear(hidden_dim // 2, 1),
            nn.Sigmoid(),
        )

    def forward(self, x):
        return self.net(x).squeeze(-1)


def train_neural_dac_fraction(
    trajectories,
    labels,
    train_idx,
    val_idx,
    test_idx,
    train_fraction,
    lr,
    epochs,
    batch_size,
    hidden_dim,
    weight_decay,
    dataset_name,
    frac_key,
):
    np.random.seed(42)
    n_train_subset = max(int(len(train_idx) * train_fraction), 2 * batch_size)
    subset_mask = np.random.choice(len(train_idx), size=n_train_subset, replace=False)
    train_idx_subset = train_idx[subset_mask]

    X_traj = torch.tensor(trajectories, dtype=torch.float32)
    y_all = labels.astype(np.float32)
    X_train = X_traj[train_idx_subset].to(device)
    X_val = X_traj[val_idx].to(device)
    X_test = X_traj[test_idx].to(device)
    y_train = torch.tensor(y_all[train_idx_subset], dtype=torch.float32).to(device)
    y_val_np = y_all[val_idx]
    y_test_np = y_all[test_idx]

    criterion = nn.BCELoss()
    train_dataset = TensorDataset(X_train, y_train)
    actual_batch = min(batch_size, max(len(train_idx_subset) // 2, 4))
    train_loader = DataLoader(train_dataset, batch_size=actual_batch, shuffle=True)

    torch.manual_seed(42)
    model = DACNet(input_dim=X_traj.shape[1], hidden_dim=hidden_dim).to(device)
    optimizer = torch.optim.Adam(model.parameters(), lr=lr, weight_decay=weight_decay)
    scheduler = torch.optim.lr_scheduler.CosineAnnealingLR(optimizer, T_max=epochs)

    best_val_auroc = 0.0
    best_test_auroc = 0.0
    best_test_pred = None

    train_losses, val_losses, train_aurocs, val_aurocs = [], [], [], []

    for epoch in range(epochs):
        model.train()
        epoch_loss = 0.0
        for X_batch, y_batch in train_loader:
            optimizer.zero_grad()
            pred = model(X_batch)
            loss = criterion(pred, y_batch)
            loss.backward()
            optimizer.step()
            epoch_loss += loss.item() * len(y_batch)
        epoch_loss /= len(train_idx_subset)
        scheduler.step()

        model.eval()
        with torch.no_grad():
            val_pred = model(X_val).cpu().numpy()
            train_pred_all = model(X_train).cpu().numpy()
            test_pred = model(X_test).cpu().numpy()
            val_loss = criterion(torch.tensor(val_pred), torch.tensor(y_val_np)).item()

        try:
            train_auroc = roc_auc_score(y_all[train_idx_subset], train_pred_all)
            val_auroc = roc_auc_score(y_val_np, val_pred)
            test_auroc = roc_auc_score(y_test_np, test_pred)
        except Exception:
            train_auroc, val_auroc, test_auroc = 0.5, 0.5, 0.5

        train_losses.append(epoch_loss)
        val_losses.append(val_loss)
        train_aurocs.append(train_auroc)
        val_aurocs.append(val_auroc)

        experiment_data[frac_key][dataset_name]["metrics"]["train"].append(
            float(train_auroc)
        )
        experiment_data[frac_key][dataset_name]["metrics"]["val"].append(
            float(val_auroc)
        )
        experiment_data[frac_key][dataset_name]["losses"]["train"].append(
            float(epoch_loss)
        )
        experiment_data[frac_key][dataset_name]["losses"]["val"].append(float(val_loss))

        if val_auroc > best_val_auroc:
            best_val_auroc = val_auroc
            best_test_auroc = test_auroc
            best_test_pred = test_pred.copy()

    experiment_data[frac_key][dataset_name]["predictions"] = (
        best_test_pred.tolist() if best_test_pred is not None else []
    )
    experiment_data[frac_key][dataset_name]["ground_truth"] = y_test_np.tolist()
    experiment_data[frac_key][dataset_name]["n_train_used"] = [n_train_subset]

    return best_test_auroc, n_train_subset


def compute_baselines(trajectories, labels, test_idx):
    single_turn_conf = trajectories[:, 0]
    drift_variance = np.var(trajectories, axis=1)
    auroc_single = roc_auc_score(labels[test_idx], -single_turn_conf[test_idx])
    auroc_drift = roc_auc_score(labels[test_idx], drift_variance[test_idx])
    return auroc_single, auroc_drift


def compute_dac_logreg_fraction(
    trajectories, labels, train_idx, val_idx, test_idx, train_fraction
):
    np.random.seed(42)
    n_train_subset = max(int(len(train_idx) * train_fraction), 10)
    subset_mask = np.random.choice(len(train_idx), size=n_train_subset, replace=False)
    train_idx_subset = train_idx[subset_mask]

    (
        single_turn_conf,
        drift_variance,
        drift_range,
        drift_std,
        drift_mean,
        drift_trend,
        autocorrs,
    ) = extract_features(trajectories)
    feature_matrix = np.column_stack(
        [
            single_turn_conf,
            drift_variance,
            drift_range,
            drift_std,
            drift_mean,
            drift_trend,
            autocorrs,
        ]
    )
    scaler = StandardScaler()
    X_train_lr = scaler.fit_transform(feature_matrix[train_idx_subset])
    X_test_lr = scaler.transform(feature_matrix[test_idx])
    y_train_lr = labels[train_idx_subset]
    y_test_lr = labels[test_idx]
    try:
        dac_model = LogisticRegression(C=0.5, max_iter=1000, random_state=42)
        dac_model.fit(X_train_lr, y_train_lr)
        dac_test_proba = dac_model.predict_proba(X_test_lr)[:, 1]
        auroc = roc_auc_score(y_test_lr, dac_test_proba)
    except Exception:
        auroc = 0.5
    return auroc


# ─────────────────────────────────────────────
# Run ablation across datasets and training fractions
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────
datasets_config = [
    ("synthetic_qa", "standard", 42),
    ("halueval_sim", "halueval", 123),
    ("sciq_sim", "sciq", 456),
]

experiment_results = {}

for ds_name, ds_type, ds_seed in datasets_config:
    print(f"\n{'='*65}")
    print(f"Training Data Size Ablation: {ds_name} (type={ds_type})")
    print(f"{'='*65}")

    labels, trajectories = generate_dataset(
        N_CLAIMS, N_TURNS, HALLUCINATION_RATE, dataset_type=ds_type, seed=ds_seed
    )
    train_idx, val_idx, test_idx = split_data(N_CLAIMS, seed=ds_seed)
    print(
        f"Total train pool: {len(train_idx)}, Val: {len(val_idx)}, Test: {len(test_idx)}"
    )

    auroc_single, auroc_drift = compute_baselines(trajectories, labels, test_idx)
    print(f"Single-Turn AUROC (no training needed): {auroc_single:.4f}")
    print(f"Drift Variance AUROC (no training needed): {auroc_drift:.4f}")

    neural_aurocs = []
    logreg_aurocs = []
    actual_sizes = []

    for frac in TRAIN_FRACTIONS:
        frac_key = f"train_fraction_{int(frac*100)}pct"
        print(f"\n  Training fraction: {frac:.0%}")

        neural_auroc, n_used = train_neural_dac_fraction(
            trajectories,
            labels,
            train_idx,
            val_idx,
            test_idx,
            train_fraction=frac,
            lr=LR,
            epochs=EPOCHS,
            batch_size=BATCH_SIZE,
            hidden_dim=HIDDEN_DIM,
            weight_decay=WEIGHT_DECAY,
            dataset_name=ds_name,
            frac_key=frac_key,
        )
        neural_aurocs.append(neural_auroc)
        actual_sizes.append(n_used)
        print(f"    Neural DAC AUROC (n_train={n_used}): {neural_auroc:.4f}")

        lr_auroc = compute_dac_logreg_fraction(
            trajectories, labels, train_idx, val_idx, test_idx, train_fraction=frac
        )
        logreg_aurocs.append(lr_auroc)
        print(
            f"    DAC LogReg AUROC (n_train={int(max(len(train_idx)*frac, 10))}): {lr_auroc:.4f}"
        )

        # Store baseline and auroc info in experiment_data
        experiment_data[frac_key][ds_name]["auroc_single_turn"] = [auroc_single]
        experiment_data[frac_key][ds_name]["auroc_drift"] = [auroc_drift]
        experiment_data[frac_key][ds_name]["auroc_dac"] = [lr_auroc]
        experiment_data[frac_key][ds_name]["auroc_neural"] = [neural_auroc]

    experiment_results[ds_name] = {
        "train_fractions": TRAIN_FRACTIONS,
        "actual_sizes": actual_sizes,
        "neural_aurocs": neural_aurocs,
        "logreg_aurocs": logreg_aurocs,
        "auroc_single": auroc_single,
        "auroc_drift": auroc_drift,
    }

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────
# Print Summary
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────
print("\n" + "=" * 70)
print("TRAINING DATA SIZE ABLATION - SUMMARY")
print("=" * 70)
for ds_name, res in experiment_results.items():
    print(f"\n[{ds_name}]")
    print(f"  Baseline Single-Turn AUROC: {res['auroc_single']:.4f}")
    print(f"  Baseline Drift Var AUROC:   {res['auroc_drift']:.4f}")
    print(
        f"  {'Fraction':>10} | {'N Train':>8} | {'Neural DAC':>12} | {'LogReg DAC':>12}"
    )
    print(f"  {'-'*50}")
    for frac, n, n_auc, lr_auc in zip(
        res["train_fractions"],
        res["actual_sizes"],
        res["neural_aurocs"],
        res["logreg_aurocs"],
    ):
        print(f"  {frac:>10.0%} | {n:>8d} | {n_auc:>12.4f} | {lr_auc:>12.4f}")

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────
# Visualizations
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────
# Plot 1: Learning curves per dataset
fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 3, figsize=(18, 5))
for ax, (ds_name, res) in zip(axes, experiment_results.items()):
    fracs_pct = [f * 100 for f in res["train_fractions"]]
    ax.plot(
        fracs_pct,
        res["neural_aurocs"],
        "b-o",
        label="Neural DAC",
        linewidth=2,
        markersize=8,
    )
    ax.plot(
        fracs_pct,
        res["logreg_aurocs"],
        "g-s",
        label="DAC LogReg",
        linewidth=2,
        markersize=8,
    )
    ax.axhline(
        y=res["auroc_single"],
        color="gray",
        linestyle="--",
        linewidth=1.5,
        label="Single-Turn",
    )
    ax.axhline(
        y=res["auroc_drift"],
        color="orange",
        linestyle="--",
        linewidth=1.5,
        label="Drift Var",
    )
    ax.set_xlabel("Training Data Used (%)", fontsize=11)
    ax.set_ylabel("Test AUROC", fontsize=11)
    ax.set_title(f"Data Efficiency: {ds_name}", fontsize=12, fontweight="bold")
    ax.legend(fontsize=9)
    ax.grid(True, alpha=0.3)
    ax.set_ylim(0.4, 1.05)
    ax.set_xlim(0, 105)
plt.suptitle(
    "Training Data Size Ablation: AUROC vs Training Fraction",
    fontsize=14,
    fontweight="bold",
)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig(
    os.path.join(working_dir, "training_data_size_ablation_curves.png"),
    dpi=150,
    bbox_inches="tight",
)
plt.close()
print("Saved: training_data_size_ablation_curves.png")

# Plot 2: Neural DAC vs. baselines across datasets (grouped by fraction)
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(14, 6))
ds_names = list(experiment_results.keys())
n_fracs = len(TRAIN_FRACTIONS)
x = np.arange(n_fracs)
width = 0.22
colors_ds = ["steelblue", "tomato", "mediumseagreen"]
for di, (ds_name, color) in enumerate(zip(ds_names, colors_ds)):
    res = experiment_results[ds_name]
    offset = (di - 1) * width
    ax.bar(
        x + offset,

ax.set_xlabel("Training Data Fraction", fontsize=12)

ax.set_title("Neural DAC AUROC vs. Training Data Fraction (All Datasets)", fontsize=13)
ax.set_xticks(x)

ax.legend(fontsize=10)
ax.set_ylim(0.4, 1.1)
ax.grid(True, alpha=0.3, axis="y")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig(
    os.path.join(working_dir, "training_data_size_ablation_bar.png"),
    dpi=150,
    bbox_inches="tight",
)
plt.close()
print("Saved: training_data_size_ablation_bar.png")

# Plot 3: Relative improvement over single-turn baseline
fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 3, figsize=(18, 5))
for ax, (ds_name, res) in zip(axes, experiment_results.items()):
    fracs_pct = [f * 100 for f in res["train_fractions"]]
    baseline = res["auroc_single"]
    neural_improvements = [(a - baseline) * 100 for a in res["neural_aurocs"]]
    logreg_improvements = [(a - baseline) * 100 for a in res["logreg_aurocs"]]

    ax.plot(
        fracs_pct,

    )

        fracs_pct,
        logreg_improvements,

        label="DAC LogReg",
        linewidth=2,
        markersize=8,
    )
    ax.axhline(
        y=drift_improvement,
        color="orange",
        linestyle="--",
        linewidth=1.5,
        label="Drift Var",
    )
    ax.axhline(y=0, color="gray", linestyle="-", linewidth=1, alpha=0.5)
    ax.fill_between(fracs_pct, neural_improvements, 0, alpha=0.15, color="blue")
    ax.set_xlabel("Training Data Used (%)", fontsize=11)
    ax.set_ylabel("AUROC Improvement over Single-Turn (%)", fontsize=10)
    ax.set_title(f"Relative Improvement: {ds_name}", fontsize=12, fontweight="bold")
    ax.legend(fontsize=9)
    ax.grid(True, alpha=0.3)
    ax.set_xlim(0, 105)
plt.suptitle(
    "Training Data Size Ablation: Improvement over Single-Turn Baseline",
    fontsize=13,
    fontweight="bold",
)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig(
    os.path.join(working_dir, "training_data_size_ablation_improvement.png"),
    dpi=150,
    bbox_inches="tight",
)
plt.close()
print("Saved: training_data_size_ablation_improvement.png")

# Plot 4: Neural DAC training curves for each fraction (per dataset)
for ds_name, res in experiment_results.items():
    fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(14, 5))
    colors = plt.cm.viridis(np.linspace(0, 1, len(TRAIN_FRACTIONS)))
    for i, frac in enumerate(TRAIN_FRACTIONS):
        frac_key = f"train_fraction_{int(frac*100)}pct"
        val_aurocs = experiment_data[frac_key][ds_name]["metrics"]["val"]
        val_losses = experiment_data[frac_key][ds_name]["losses"]["val"]
        label = f"{frac:.0%}"
        if val_aurocs:
            axes[0].plot(val_aurocs, color=colors[i], linewidth=1.5, label=label)
        if val_losses:
            axes[1].plot(val_losses, color=colors[i], linewidth=1.5, label=label)
    axes[0].set_title(f"Val AUROC by Training Fraction [{ds_name}]")
    axes[0].set_xlabel("Epoch")
    axes[0].set_ylabel("Val AUROC")
    axes[0].legend(title="Train Fraction", fontsize=9)
    axes[0].grid(True, alpha=0.3)
    axes[1].set_title(f"Val Loss by Training Fraction [{ds_name}]")
    axes[1].set_xlabel("Epoch")
    axes[1].set_ylabel("Val BCE Loss")
    axes[1].legend(title="Train Fraction", fontsize=9)
    axes[1].grid(True, alpha=0.3)
    plt.suptitle(
        f"Neural DAC Training Dynamics: {ds_name}", fontsize=13, fontweight="bold"
    )
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(
        os.path.join(working_dir, f"training_dynamics_{ds_name}.png"),
        dpi=150,
        bbox_inches="tight",
    )

    print(f"Saved: training_dynamics_{ds_name}.png")

# Save results
np.save(os.path.join(working_dir, "experiment_data.npy"), experiment_data)
np.save(
    os.path.join(working_dir, "training_data_size_ablation_results.npy"),

plot_code:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np
import os

working_dir = os.path.join(os.getcwd(), "working")

try:
    experiment_data = np.load(
        os.path.join(working_dir, "experiment_data.npy"), allow_pickle=True
    ).item()
except Exception as e:
    print(f"Error loading experiment data: {e}")

TRAIN_FRACTIONS = [0.1, 0.25, 0.5, 0.75, 1.0]
DATASETS = ["synthetic_qa", "halueval_sim", "sciq_sim"]
frac_keys = [f"train_fraction_{int(f*100)}pct" for f in TRAIN_FRACTIONS]

# Plot 1: AUROC vs Training Fraction for all methods per dataset
try:
    fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 3, figsize=(18, 5))
    for ax, ds in zip(axes, DATASETS):
        neural, logreg, single, drift = [], [], [], []
        for fk in frac_keys:
            d = experiment_data[fk][ds]
            neural.append(d["auroc_neural"][0] if d["auroc_neural"] else np.nan)
            logreg.append(d["auroc_dac"][0] if d["auroc_dac"] else np.nan)
            single.append(
                d["auroc_single_turn"][0] if d["auroc_single_turn"] else np.nan
            )
            drift.append(d["auroc_drift"][0] if d["auroc_drift"] else np.nan)
        fracs_pct = [f * 100 for f in TRAIN_FRACTIONS]
        ax.plot(fracs_pct, neural, "b-o", label="Neural DAC", linewidth=2, markersize=8)
        ax.plot(fracs_pct, logreg, "g-s", label="DAC LogReg", linewidth=2, markersize=8)
        ax.axhline(
            y=np.nanmean(single),
            color="gray",
            linestyle="--",
            linewidth=1.5,
            label="Single-Turn",
        )
        ax.axhline(
            y=np.nanmean(drift),
            color="orange",
            linestyle="--",
            linewidth=1.5,
            label="Drift Var",
        )
        ax.set_xlabel("Training Data Used (%)", fontsize=11)
        ax.set_ylabel("Test AUROC", fontsize=11)
        ax.set_title(f"Data Efficiency: {ds}", fontsize=12, fontweight="bold")
        ax.legend(fontsize=9)
        ax.grid(True, alpha=0.3)
        ax.set_ylim(0.4, 1.05)
        ax.set_xlim(0, 105)
    plt.suptitle(
        "Training Data Size Ablation: AUROC vs Training Fraction\nAll Datasets",
        fontsize=13,
        fontweight="bold",
    )
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(
        os.path.join(working_dir, "ablation_auroc_vs_fraction_all_datasets.png"),
        dpi=150,
        bbox_inches="tight",
    )
    plt.close()
    print("Saved: ablation_auroc_vs_fraction_all_datasets.png")
except Exception as e:
    print(f"Error plot1: {e}")
    plt.close()

# Plot 2: Grouped bar chart - Neural DAC AUROC across datasets and fractions
try:
    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(14, 6))
    x = np.arange(len(TRAIN_FRACTIONS))
    width = 0.25
    colors = ["steelblue", "tomato", "mediumseagreen"]
    for di, (ds, color) in enumerate(zip(DATASETS, colors)):
        vals = [
            (
                experiment_data[fk][ds]["auroc_neural"][0]
                if experiment_data[fk][ds]["auroc_neural"]
                else np.nan
            )
            for fk in frac_keys
        ]
        ax.bar(
            x + (di - 1) * width,
            vals,
            width,
            label=f"Neural DAC ({ds})",
            color=color,
            alpha=0.85,
            edgecolor="black",
        )
    ax.set_xlabel("Training Data Fraction", fontsize=12)
    ax.set_ylabel("Test AUROC (Neural DAC)", fontsize=12)
    ax.set_title(
        "Neural DAC AUROC vs Training Data Fraction\nGrouped Bar Chart Across All Datasets",
        fontsize=13,
        fontweight="bold",
    )
    ax.set_xticks(x)
    ax.set_xticklabels([f"{f:.0%}" for f in TRAIN_FRACTIONS])
    ax.legend(fontsize=10)
    ax.set_ylim(0.4, 1.1)
    ax.grid(True, alpha=0.3, axis="y")
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(
        os.path.join(
            working_dir, "ablation_neural_dac_auroc_grouped_bar_all_datasets.png"
        ),
        dpi=150,
        bbox_inches="tight",
    )
    plt.close()
    print("Saved: ablation_neural_dac_auroc_grouped_bar_all_datasets.png")
except Exception as e:
    print(f"Error plot2: {e}")
    plt.close()

# Plot 3: Relative improvement over single-turn baseline
try:
    fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 3, figsize=(18, 5))
    for ax, ds in zip(axes, DATASETS):
        neural, logreg, single_vals, drift_vals = [], [], [], []
        for fk in frac_keys:
            d = experiment_data[fk][ds]
            neural.append(d["auroc_neural"][0] if d["auroc_neural"] else np.nan)
            logreg.append(d["auroc_dac"][0] if d["auroc_dac"] else np.nan)
            single_vals.append(
                d["auroc_single_turn"][0] if d["auroc_single_turn"] else np.nan
            )
            drift_vals.append(d["auroc_drift"][0] if d["auroc_drift"] else np.nan)
        baseline = np.nanmean(single_vals)
        drift_imp = (np.nanmean(drift_vals) - baseline) * 100
        fracs_pct = [f * 100 for f in TRAIN_FRACTIONS]
        n_imp = [(v - baseline) * 100 for v in neural]
        l_imp = [(v - baseline) * 100 for v in logreg]
        ax.plot(fracs_pct, n_imp, "b-o", label="Neural DAC", linewidth=2, markersize=8)
        ax.plot(fracs_pct, l_imp, "g-s", label="DAC LogReg", linewidth=2, markersize=8)
        ax.axhline(
            y=drift_imp,
            color="orange",
            linestyle="--",
            linewidth=1.5,
            label="Drift Var",
        )
        ax.axhline(y=0, color="gray", linestyle="-", linewidth=1, alpha=0.5)
        ax.fill_between(fracs_pct, n_imp, 0, alpha=0.15, color="blue")
        ax.set_xlabel("Training Data Used (%)", fontsize=11)
        ax.set_ylabel("AUROC Improvement over Single-Turn (%)", fontsize=10)
        ax.set_title(f"Relative Improvement: {ds}", fontsize=12, fontweight="bold")
        ax.legend(fontsize=9)
        ax.grid(True, alpha=0.3)
        ax.set_xlim(0, 105)
    plt.suptitle(
        "Training Data Size Ablation: Improvement over Single-Turn Baseline\nAll Datasets",
        fontsize=13,
        fontweight="bold",
    )
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(
        os.path.join(
            working_dir,
            "ablation_relative_improvement_over_singleturn_all_datasets.png",
        ),
        dpi=150,
        bbox_inches="tight",
    )
    plt.close()
    print("Saved: ablation_relative_improvement_over_singleturn_all_datasets.png")
except Exception as e:
    print(f"Error plot3: {e}")
    plt.close()

# Plot 4: Training dynamics (val AUROC + val loss) per dataset
for ds in DATASETS:
    try:
        fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(14, 5))
        colors = plt.cm.viridis(np.linspace(0, 1, len(TRAIN_FRACTIONS)))
        for i, (frac, fk) in enumerate(zip(TRAIN_FRACTIONS, frac_keys)):
            d = experiment_data[fk][ds]
            val_aurocs = d["metrics"]["val"]
            val_losses = d["losses"]["val"]
            label = f"{frac:.0%}"
            if val_aurocs:
                axes[0].plot(val_aurocs, color=colors[i], linewidth=1.5, label=label)
            if val_losses:
                axes[1].plot(val_losses, color=colors[i], linewidth=1.5, label=label)
        axes[0].set_title(
            f"Validation AUROC by Training Fraction\nDataset: {ds}", fontsize=11
        )
        axes[0].set_xlabel("Epoch")
        axes[0].set_ylabel("Val AUROC")
        axes[0].legend(title="Train Fraction", fontsize=9)
        axes[0].grid(True, alpha=0.3)
        axes[1].set_title(
            f"Validation BCE Loss by Training Fraction\nDataset: {ds}", fontsize=11
        )
        axes[1].set_xlabel("Epoch")
        axes[1].set_ylabel("Val BCE Loss")
        axes[1].legend(title="Train Fraction", fontsize=9)
        axes[1].grid(True, alpha=0.3)
        plt.suptitle(
            f"Neural DAC Training Dynamics: {ds}", fontsize=13, fontweight="bold"
        )
        plt.tight_layout()
        plt.savefig(
            os.path.join(working_dir, f"ablation_training_dynamics_{ds}.png"),
            dpi=150,
            bbox_inches="tight",
        )
        plt.close()
        print(f"Saved: ablation_training_dynamics_{ds}.png")
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"Error training dynamics {ds}: {e}")
        plt.close()

# Plot 5: Heatmap of final AUROCs across methods, datasets, and fractions
try:
    methods = ["auroc_neural", "auroc_dac", "auroc_single_turn", "auroc_drift"]
    method_labels = ["Neural DAC", "LogReg DAC", "Single-Turn", "Drift Var"]
    fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, len(DATASETS), figsize=(18, 5))
    for ax, ds in zip(axes, DATASETS):
        matrix = np.zeros((len(methods), len(TRAIN_FRACTIONS)))
        for j, fk in enumerate(frac_keys):
            d = experiment_data[fk][ds]
            for i, m in enumerate(methods):
                matrix[i, j] = d[m][0] if d[m] else np.nan
        im = ax.imshow(matrix, aspect="auto", cmap="RdYlGn", vmin=0.4, vmax=1.0)
        ax.set_xticks(range(len(TRAIN_FRACTIONS)))
        ax.set_xticklabels([f"{f:.0%}" for f in TRAIN_FRACTIONS], fontsize=9)
        ax.set_yticks(range(len(methods)))
        ax.set_yticklabels(method_labels, fontsize=10)
        ax.set_xlabel("Training Fraction", fontsize=10)
        ax.set_title(f"Dataset: {ds}", fontsize=11, fontweight="bold")
        for i in range(len(methods)):
            for j in range(len(TRAIN_FRACTIONS)):
                ax.text(
                    j,
                    i,
                    f"{matrix[i,j]:.3f}",
                    ha="center",
                    va="center",
                    fontsize=8,
                    color="black",
                )
        plt.colorbar(im, ax=ax, shrink=0.8)
    plt.suptitle(
        "AUROC Heatmap: All Methods × Datasets × Training Fractions",
        fontsize=13,
        fontweight="bold",
    )
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(
        os.path.join(working_dir, "ablation_auroc_heatmap_all_methods_datasets.png"),
        dpi=150,
        bbox_inches="tight",
    )
    plt.close()
    print("Saved: ablation_auroc_heatmap_all_methods_datasets.png")
except Exception as e:
    print(f"Error heatmap: {e}")
    plt.close()
plot_plan: The experiment runs a training data size ablation across three datasets (synthetic_qa, halueval_sim, sciq_sim) and five training fractions (10%, 25%, 50%, 75%, 100%), training a Neural DAC model and a Logistic Regression DAC baseline, plus computing single-turn and drift variance baselines. The experiment_data.npy stores per-fraction, per-dataset metrics including train/val AUROC curves, losses, and final AUROC scores for all methods. I'll create plots covering: (1) AUROC vs training fraction lines per dataset, (2) a grouped bar chart of Neural DAC AUROC across datasets, (3) relative improvement over single-turn baseline, (4) training dynamics (val AUROC and loss curves) per dataset, and (5) a heatmap summarizing final AUROCs across all methods, datasets, and fractions. Each plot will be in its own try-except block and saved with descriptive names.

plot_analyses.analysis:
AUROC heatmaps across methods × datasets × training fractions show DAC variants and drift-only features saturating near-perfect discrimination, while the single-turn baseline is markedly worse on some datasets.

- synthetic_qa: Single-Turn is flat at 0.867 across all training fractions. Drift Var alone is 0.995 (also flat), indicating drift variance is already an extremely strong hallucination signal without any learned combination. LogReg DAC is 0.995 at 10% and ~0.999 thereafter; Neural DAC starts much lower at 10% (0.909) but rapidly catches up (0.990–0.998 by 25–100%). This suggests the neural combiner is data-hungry and underfits at small data, whereas linear/logistic combination is data-efficient.

- halueval_sim: Single-Turn is 0.746 (flat), but Drift Var is 1.000 at all fractions; LogReg DAC is also 1.000 throughout; Neural DAC is 0.994–1.000, reaching 1.000 by 50%+. This dataset appears almost perfectly separable by drift variance alone, raising the possibility of dataset artifacts (e.g., drift variance correlates strongly with label due to construction).

- sciq_sim: Single-Turn is already very high (0.993 flat). Drift Var and both DACs are 1.000 across all fractions (Neural DAC shows 0.999 at 25%). Gains over baseline are therefore necessarily tiny; this dataset may be too easy / near-ceiling for AUROC to be informative.

Implications for ablation goals:
- The “drift” component seems to dominate: Drift Var alone matches or nearly matches learned DAC in all datasets, especially halueval_sim and sciq_sim.
- The “calibration combiner” (Neural vs LogReg) matters mainly in low-data regimes and only on synthetic_qa; elsewhere everything is saturated.
- Because many cells are 1.000, AUROC may be insufficiently sensitive; consider adding AUPRC, ECE/Brier, selective risk-coverage, and evaluating on harder splits or cross-domain generalization.

Grouped bar chart (Neural DAC AUROC vs training fraction across datasets) emphasizes data efficiency differences and ceiling effects.

- synthetic_qa: Neural DAC rises from ~0.91 at 10% to ~0.99 by 25% and approaches ~1.00 by 100%. The large jump from 10%→25% indicates the neural model needs a minimum sample size to learn a stable mapping; below that it likely over-regularizes or fails to learn interactions.

- halueval_sim and sciq_sim: Neural DAC is ~0.995–1.00 even at 10%, and essentially 1.00 thereafter. This reinforces that these datasets are near-separable with the available features; additional training data yields negligible benefit.

Takeaway: If your paper argues DAC is a “lightweight post-hoc method,” the neural variant may be unnecessary; the linear/logistic variant looks more data-efficient and comparably strong. The neural variant’s main value would be if it improves calibration metrics (not shown) or generalizes better to distribution shifts.

Relative improvement over Single-Turn baseline vs training data used clarifies where gains come from and whether training size matters.

- synthetic_qa: Neural DAC improvement is small at 10% (~4% AUROC improvement) but jumps to ~12–13% from 25% onward. LogReg DAC and Drift Var are consistently ~13% improvement across all fractions, indicating (i) most of the gain is attributable to drift variance itself, and (ii) learning a combiner is not crucial once drift is included.

- halueval_sim: Neural DAC is ~25% improvement at 10–25% and ~25.5% by 50–100%. LogReg DAC and Drift Var sit at ~25.5% across all fractions. Again, drift variance alone explains essentially all improvement; training fraction barely matters.

- sciq_sim: Improvements are tiny (~0.62–0.68%) because the baseline is already 0.993 AUROC. Neural DAC fluctuates slightly with fraction; LogReg DAC and Drift Var are flat at ~0.67–0.68%.

Interpretation: The ablation suggests the “drift” feature is the key contributor; the “DAC learning” step adds marginal value except when the neural model is undertrained (synthetic_qa at 10%). This is a strong result if you position drift as a new signal, but it weakens claims about the necessity of DAC unless you show benefits on calibration/selection or on harder datasets.

AUROC vs training fraction line plots (data efficiency) show saturation and highlight that single-turn baselines are constant with training fraction (as expected).

- synthetic_qa: Neural DAC improves from ~0.91 (10%) to ~0.99 (25%) and then plateaus just below 1.0; LogReg DAC is ~0.999–1.0 across all fractions; Drift Var is ~0.995 flat. The ordering suggests: LogReg DAC ≳ Drift Var > Neural DAC at low data; Neural catches up with more data.

- halueval_sim: All drift-based methods are ~1.0 across fractions; single-turn stays ~0.746. This is a red flag for potential leakage/artifact: if drift variance alone perfectly separates labels, the dataset may encode the label in the conversational structure or in how “no new info” turns are constructed.

- sciq_sim: Everything is ~1.0; single-turn is ~0.993. Ceiling effects dominate.

Recommendation: Add a “hard” evaluation where drift variance is not trivially separable: e.g., adversarially match drift statistics between hallucinated and non-hallucinated examples, or evaluate on naturally occurring multi-turn logs where drift may be subtler.

Neural DAC AUROC vs weight decay indicates the model is insensitive to regularization within the tested range.

- synthetic_qa: AUROC varies minimally (0.9972–0.9974) from WD=0 to 1e-3. Differences are in the 4th decimal place, suggesting either (i) the task is easy at this operating point, (ii) the model capacity is small relative to data, or (iii) the metric is saturated.

- halueval_sim and sciq_sim: AUROC is exactly 1.0000 for all weight decays. This provides no guidance for hyperparameter tuning and again suggests separability/ceiling.

Implication for ablation: You can claim robustness to weight decay, but it’s not very informative given saturation. Consider reporting validation loss/ECE sensitivity to WD, or testing much larger WD / dropout / early stopping to see if calibration changes even when AUROC stays at 1.0.

Weight decay × dataset heatmap reiterates the same: near-constant AUROC across WD settings.

- synthetic_qa shows tiny fluctuations around 0.9973; halueval_sim and sciq_sim are 1.0000 everywhere.

Takeaway: This supports that the method is not brittle to WD, but also suggests the evaluation regime is too easy to surface meaningful regularization effects. If the goal is component analysis, you may get more insight by ablating input features (single-turn confidence only vs drift-only vs combined; per-turn positions; variance vs range vs monotonic drift) rather than optimizer hyperparameters.

Repeated training-data-size AUROC vs fraction figure (same as earlier) confirms the same saturation patterns; no additional distinct signal beyond prior plot.

Use this redundancy to streamline the paper: include one clear data-efficiency plot and move duplicates to appendix.

Repeated Neural DAC bar chart (same as earlier) again shows Neural DAC needs more data on synthetic_qa but is near-ceiling on halueval_sim/sciq_sim.

If you keep one, prefer the version that includes numerical labels or error bars; otherwise remove duplication.

Training dynamics on halueval_sim (Val AUROC and Val BCE loss vs epoch) reveal potential overfitting/instability at small training fractions and fast convergence at larger fractions.

- Val AUROC: 100% fraction quickly rises to ~0.999–1.000 and stays there. 50% and 75% climb to ~0.993–0.996 with some noise. 10% and 25% peak early (~0.978–0.979) then gradually decline to ~0.975 (10%) and ~0.968 (25%), suggesting overfitting after early epochs.

- Val loss: All fractions drop sharply early. For 10% and 25%, loss bottoms out then increases over time (classic overfitting), ending around ~0.15 (10%) and ~0.22 (25%). For 50–100%, loss stays low (~0.05–0.08) with mild noise.

Actionable: Use early stopping based on val loss/AUROC for low-data regimes; report best-epoch performance rather than final epoch. Also consider stronger regularization or smaller model for 10–25% to prevent the AUROC decay. This plot is one of the few that actually shows a meaningful training-size effect beyond saturation.

Training dynamics on synthetic_qa show strong dependence on training fraction and clear convergence behavior.

- Val AUROC: 100% reaches ~0.999–1.000 rapidly and stays. 50% and 75% quickly reach ~0.99+ and remain high. 25% improves steadily to ~0.985–0.99 by late epochs. 10% rises slowly from ~0.84 to ~0.92 and then plateaus, indicating limited learnability with very small data.

- Val loss: Higher fractions achieve much lower loss (100% down to ~0.04–0.06; 50% ~0.08–0.10; 75% ~0.11–0.13). 25% decreases to ~0.15. 10% remains high (~0.41) and decreases only modestly, consistent with underfitting or insufficient signal.

Interpretation: Unlike halueval_sim where drift seems nearly perfectly predictive, synthetic_qa appears to require learning to combine signals (or at least benefits from more data). This makes synthetic_qa the most informative dataset for ablations; consider emphasizing it for component analysis and using halueval_sim/sciq_sim as “easy/ceiling” cases or revising them to be harder.

vlm_feedback_summary: Across datasets and ablations, drift-based signals (especially Drift Var) dominate performance: they nearly match or equal DAC variants and often achieve perfect AUROC, while single-turn confidence is much weaker on synthetic_qa and halueval_sim. LogReg DAC is extremely data-efficient and matches Drift Var at small fractions; Neural DAC needs more data on synthetic_qa and shows overfitting at low fractions in halueval_sim training dynamics. Weight decay has negligible effect due to metric saturation. Several datasets exhibit ceiling effects (AUROC≈1.0), suggesting potential artifacts or overly easy evaluation; additional metrics (AUPRC/ECE/selective risk) and harder or more naturalistic splits are needed to substantiate claims about the necessity of DAC beyond the drift feature itself.

overall_plan: The research focuses on developing and validating a Neural DAC (neural network-based detector) model for hallucination detection tasks. The work began with fixing critical implementation bugs: (1) a shared data loader issue between LR=1e-3 and LR=1e-4 model configurations, (2) insufficient training epochs for the LR=1e-4 model (requiring 100-200 epochs for proper convergence), and (3) the need to introduce two new synthetic datasets inspired by HaluEval and SciQ to simulate different hallucination patterns. The bug fixes also involved creating separate data loaders per configuration and tuning hyperparameters (batch size, hidden dim, weight decay). This established a proper three-dataset experimental infrastructure: synthetic_qa, halueval_sim, and sciq_sim. Building on this corrected foundation, the current plan conducts an ablation study ('optimizer_type_ablation') that systematically compares four optimizers—Adam, AdamW, SGD with momentum, and RMSprop—on the Neural DAC model across all three datasets. The ablation generates datasets once, trains each optimizer-dataset combination, and stores metrics in experiment_data. Visualizations include per-dataset training curves, cross-dataset bar charts, and validation AUROC comparison plots, with results saved as experiment_data.npy. The overall scientific objective is to understand how optimizer choice affects hallucination detection performance across diverse dataset types.

metric_names.description: AUROC score on the training set, reported per optimizer. Final and best values captured across optimizers (adam, adamw, sgd_momentum, rmsprop).
AUROC score on the validation set, reported per optimizer. Final and best values captured across optimizers (adam, adamw, sgd_momentum, rmsprop).

import os

working_dir = os.path.join(os.getcwd(), "working")
os.makedirs(working_dir, exist_ok=True)

import numpy as np
import torch
import torch.nn as nn
from torch.utils.data import DataLoader, TensorDataset
from sklearn.metrics import roc_auc_score
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import warnings

warnings.filterwarnings("ignore")

device = torch.device("cuda" if torch.cuda.is_available() else "cpu")
print(f"Using device: {device}")

np.random.seed(42)
torch.manual_seed(42)

N_CLAIMS = 1000
N_TURNS = 8
HALLUCINATION_RATE = 0.4
EPOCHS = 150
BATCH_SIZE = 32
HIDDEN_DIM = 128
LR = 1e-3
OPTIMIZERS = ["adam", "adamw", "sgd_momentum", "rmsprop"]

experiment_data = {
    opt: {
        "synthetic_qa": {
            "metrics": {"train": [], "val": []},
            "losses": {"train": [], "val": []},
            "predictions": [],
            "ground_truth": [],
            "auroc_neural": [],
        },
        "halueval_sim": {
            "metrics": {"train": [], "val": []},
            "losses": {"train": [], "val": []},
            "predictions": [],
            "ground_truth": [],
            "auroc_neural": [],
        },
        "sciq_sim": {
            "metrics": {"train": [], "val": []},
            "losses": {"train": [], "val": []},
            "predictions": [],
            "ground_truth": [],
            "auroc_neural": [],
        },
    }
    for opt in OPTIMIZERS
}


def generate_confidence_trajectory(is_hallucinated, n_turns, dataset_type="standard"):
    if dataset_type == "halueval":
        if is_hallucinated:
            base_conf = np.random.uniform(0.6, 0.9)
            drift_magnitude = np.random.uniform(0.2, 0.4)
            noise_std = 0.12
            trajectory = [base_conf]
            for t in range(1, n_turns):
                step = np.random.normal(-0.05, drift_magnitude / np.sqrt(n_turns))
                reversion = -0.15 * (trajectory[-1] - base_conf * 0.5)
                new_val = (
                    trajectory[-1] + step + reversion + np.random.normal(0, noise_std)
                )
                trajectory.append(np.clip(new_val, 0.05, 0.99))
        else:
            base_conf = np.random.uniform(0.70, 0.97)
            drift_magnitude = np.random.uniform(0.01, 0.06)
            noise_std = 0.025
            trajectory = [base_conf]
            for t in range(1, n_turns):
                step = np.random.normal(0, drift_magnitude / np.sqrt(n_turns))
                reversion = -0.35 * (trajectory[-1] - base_conf)
                new_val = (
                    trajectory[-1] + step + reversion + np.random.normal(0, noise_std)
                )
                trajectory.append(np.clip(new_val, 0.05, 0.99))
    elif dataset_type == "sciq":
        if is_hallucinated:
            base_conf = np.random.uniform(0.35, 0.80)
            drift_magnitude = np.random.uniform(0.18, 0.38)
            noise_std = 0.11
            trajectory = [base_conf]
            for t in range(1, n_turns):
                step = np.random.normal(0, drift_magnitude / np.sqrt(n_turns))
                reversion = -0.1 * (trajectory[-1] - base_conf)
                new_val = (
                    trajectory[-1] + step + reversion + np.random.normal(0, noise_std)
                )
                trajectory.append(np.clip(new_val, 0.05, 0.99))
        else:
            base_conf = np.random.uniform(0.75, 0.99)
            drift_magnitude = np.random.uniform(0.01, 0.05)
            noise_std = 0.02
            trajectory = [base_conf]
            for t in range(1, n_turns):
                step = np.random.normal(0, drift_magnitude / np.sqrt(n_turns))
                reversion = -0.4 * (trajectory[-1] - base_conf)
                new_val = (
                    trajectory[-1] + step + reversion + np.random.normal(0, noise_std)
                )
                trajectory.append(np.clip(new_val, 0.05, 0.99))
    else:
        if is_hallucinated:
            base_conf = np.random.uniform(0.4, 0.85)
            drift_magnitude = np.random.uniform(0.15, 0.35)
            noise_std = 0.10
            trajectory = [base_conf]
            for t in range(1, n_turns):
                step = np.random.normal(0, drift_magnitude / np.sqrt(n_turns))
                reversion = -0.2 * (trajectory[-1] - base_conf)
                new_val = (
                    trajectory[-1] + step + reversion + np.random.normal(0, noise_std)
                )
                trajectory.append(np.clip(new_val, 0.05, 0.99))
        else:
            base_conf = np.random.uniform(0.65, 0.97)
            drift_magnitude = np.random.uniform(0.02, 0.08)
            noise_std = 0.03
            trajectory = [base_conf]
            for t in range(1, n_turns):
                step = np.random.normal(0, drift_magnitude / np.sqrt(n_turns))
                reversion = -0.3 * (trajectory[-1] - base_conf)
                new_val = (
                    trajectory[-1] + step + reversion + np.random.normal(0, noise_std)
                )
                trajectory.append(np.clip(new_val, 0.05, 0.99))
    return np.array(trajectory)


def generate_dataset(
    n_claims, n_turns, hallucination_rate, dataset_type="standard", seed=42
):
    np.random.seed(seed)
    labels = (np.random.rand(n_claims) < hallucination_rate).astype(int)
    trajectories = np.array(
        [
            generate_confidence_trajectory(
                labels[i], n_turns, dataset_type=dataset_type
            )
            for i in range(n_claims)
        ]
    )
    return labels, trajectories


def split_data(n_claims, seed=42):
    np.random.seed(seed)
    n_train = int(0.6 * n_claims)
    n_val = int(0.2 * n_claims)
    idx = np.arange(n_claims)
    np.random.shuffle(idx)
    return idx[:n_train], idx[n_train : n_train + n_val], idx[n_train + n_val :]


class DACNet(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, input_dim, hidden_dim=128):
        super().__init__()
        self.net = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Linear(input_dim, hidden_dim),
            nn.BatchNorm1d(hidden_dim),
            nn.ReLU(),
            nn.Dropout(0.3),
            nn.Linear(hidden_dim, hidden_dim // 2),
            nn.ReLU(),
            nn.Dropout(0.2),
            nn.Linear(hidden_dim // 2, 1),
            nn.Sigmoid(),
        )

    def forward(self, x):
        return self.net(x).squeeze(-1)


def get_optimizer(opt_name, parameters, lr):
    if opt_name == "adam":
        return torch.optim.Adam(parameters, lr=lr, weight_decay=1e-4)
    elif opt_name == "adamw":
        return torch.optim.AdamW(parameters, lr=lr, weight_decay=1e-2)
    elif opt_name == "sgd_momentum":
        return torch.optim.SGD(
            parameters, lr=lr, momentum=0.9, weight_decay=1e-4, nesterov=True
        )
    elif opt_name == "rmsprop":
        return torch.optim.RMSprop(parameters, lr=lr, weight_decay=1e-4, momentum=0.9)
    else:
        raise ValueError(f"Unknown optimizer: {opt_name}")


def train_neural_dac(
    trajectories,
    labels,
    train_idx,
    val_idx,
    test_idx,
    opt_name,
    lr,
    epochs,
    batch_size=32,
    hidden_dim=128,
    dataset_name="synthetic_qa",
):
    X_traj = torch.tensor(trajectories, dtype=torch.float32)
    y_all = labels.astype(np.float32)
    X_train = X_traj[train_idx].to(device)
    X_val = X_traj[val_idx].to(device)
    X_test = X_traj[test_idx].to(device)
    y_train = torch.tensor(y_all[train_idx], dtype=torch.float32).to(device)
    y_val_np = y_all[val_idx]
    y_test_np = y_all[test_idx]
    criterion = nn.BCELoss()
    train_dataset = TensorDataset(X_train, y_train)
    train_loader = DataLoader(train_dataset, batch_size=batch_size, shuffle=True)
    torch.manual_seed(42)
    model = DACNet(input_dim=X_traj.shape[1], hidden_dim=hidden_dim).to(device)
    optimizer = get_optimizer(opt_name, model.parameters(), lr)
    scheduler = torch.optim.lr_scheduler.CosineAnnealingLR(optimizer, T_max=epochs)
    train_losses, val_losses, train_aurocs, val_aurocs = [], [], [], []
    for epoch in range(epochs):
        model.train()
        epoch_loss = 0.0
        for X_batch, y_batch in train_loader:
            optimizer.zero_grad()
            pred = model(X_batch)
            loss = criterion(pred, y_batch)
            loss.backward()
            optimizer.step()
            epoch_loss += loss.item() * len(y_batch)
        epoch_loss /= len(train_idx)
        scheduler.step()
        model.eval()
        with torch.no_grad():
            val_pred = model(X_val).cpu().numpy()
            train_pred_all = model(X_train).cpu().numpy()
            val_loss = criterion(torch.tensor(val_pred), torch.tensor(y_val_np)).item()
            train_auroc_nn = roc_auc_score(y_all[train_idx], train_pred_all)
            val_auroc_nn = roc_auc_score(y_val_np, val_pred)
        train_losses.append(epoch_loss)
        val_losses.append(val_loss)
        train_aurocs.append(train_auroc_nn)
        val_aurocs.append(val_auroc_nn)
        experiment_data[opt_name][dataset_name]["metrics"]["train"].append(
            float(train_auroc_nn)
        )
        experiment_data[opt_name][dataset_name]["metrics"]["val"].append(
            float(val_auroc_nn)
        )
        experiment_data[opt_name][dataset_name]["losses"]["train"].append(
            float(epoch_loss)
        )
        experiment_data[opt_name][dataset_name]["losses"]["val"].append(float(val_loss))
        if (epoch + 1) % 30 == 0:
            print(
                f"  [{opt_name}] Epoch {epoch+1:3d}: val_loss={val_loss:.4f}, val_auroc={val_auroc_nn:.4f}"
            )
    model.eval()
    with torch.no_grad():
        test_pred = model(X_test).cpu().numpy()
    auroc_test = roc_auc_score(y_test_np, test_pred)
    experiment_data[opt_name][dataset_name]["predictions"] = test_pred.tolist()
    experiment_data[opt_name][dataset_name]["ground_truth"] = y_test_np.tolist()
    experiment_data[opt_name][dataset_name]["auroc_neural"] = [auroc_test]
    return auroc_test, test_pred, train_losses, val_losses, train_aurocs, val_aurocs


# Generate datasets
datasets_config = [
    ("synthetic_qa", "standard", 42),
    ("halueval_sim", "halueval", 123),
    ("sciq_sim", "sciq", 456),
]

all_datasets = {}
for ds_name, ds_type, ds_seed in datasets_config:
    labels, trajectories = generate_dataset(
        N_CLAIMS, N_TURNS, HALLUCINATION_RATE, dataset_type=ds_type, seed=ds_seed
    )
    train_idx, val_idx, test_idx = split_data(N_CLAIMS, seed=ds_seed)
    all_datasets[ds_name] = dict(
        labels=labels,
        trajectories=trajectories,
        train_idx=train_idx,
        val_idx=val_idx,
        test_idx=test_idx,
    )
    print(f"[{ds_name}] Hallucinated: {labels.sum()}, Correct: {(1-labels).sum()}")

# Run ablation
results_auroc = {opt: {} for opt in OPTIMIZERS}
results_train_l = {opt: {} for opt in OPTIMIZERS}
results_val_l = {opt: {} for opt in OPTIMIZERS}
results_train_a = {opt: {} for opt in OPTIMIZERS}
results_val_a = {opt: {} for opt in OPTIMIZERS}

for opt_name in OPTIMIZERS:
    for ds_name, ds_type, ds_seed in datasets_config:
        print(f"\n{'='*55}")
        print(f"Optimizer: {opt_name} | Dataset: {ds_name}")
        print(f"{'='*55}")
        ds = all_datasets[ds_name]
        auroc, pred, tl, vl, ta, va = train_neural_dac(
            ds["trajectories"],
            ds["labels"],
            ds["train_idx"],
            ds["val_idx"],
            ds["test_idx"],
            opt_name=opt_name,
            lr=LR,
            epochs=EPOCHS,
            batch_size=BATCH_SIZE,
            hidden_dim=HIDDEN_DIM,
            dataset_name=ds_name,
        )
        results_auroc[opt_name][ds_name] = auroc
        results_train_l[opt_name][ds_name] = tl
        results_val_l[opt_name][ds_name] = vl
        results_train_a[opt_name][ds_name] = ta
        results_val_a[opt_name][ds_name] = va
        print(f"  => Test AUROC: {auroc:.4f}")

# Summary
print("\n" + "=" * 65)
print("OPTIMIZER ABLATION — RESULTS SUMMARY (Test AUROC)")
print("=" * 65)
header = f"{'Dataset':<18}" + "".join(f"{o:<16}" for o in OPTIMIZERS)
print(header)
print("-" * 65)
for ds_name, _, _ in datasets_config:
    row = f"{ds_name:<18}"
    for opt_name in OPTIMIZERS:
        row += f"{results_auroc[opt_name][ds_name]:<16.4f}"
    print(row)

# Visualizations
opt_colors = {
    "adam": "blue",
    "adamw": "green",
    "sgd_momentum": "red",
    "rmsprop": "purple",
}

# Per-dataset training curves
for ds_name, _, _ in datasets_config:
    fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(14, 5))
    for opt_name in OPTIMIZERS:
        c = opt_colors[opt_name]
        axes[0].plot(
            results_train_l[opt_name][ds_name],
            color=c,
            linestyle="-",
            alpha=0.8,
            label=f"{opt_name} Train",
        )
        axes[0].plot(
            results_val_l[opt_name][ds_name],
            color=c,
            linestyle="--",
            alpha=0.8,
            label=f"{opt_name} Val",
        )
        axes[1].plot(
            results_train_a[opt_name][ds_name],
            color=c,
            linestyle="-",
            alpha=0.8,
            label=f"{opt_name} Train",
        )
        axes[1].plot(
            results_val_a[opt_name][ds_name],
            color=c,
            linestyle="--",
            alpha=0.8,
            label=f"{opt_name} Val",
        )
    axes[0].set_title(f"Loss Curves [{ds_name}]")
    axes[0].set_xlabel("Epoch")
    axes[0].set_ylabel("BCE Loss")
    axes[0].legend(fontsize=7)
    axes[0].grid(True, alpha=0.3)
    axes[1].set_title(f"AUROC Curves [{ds_name}]")
    axes[1].set_xlabel("Epoch")
    axes[1].set_ylabel("AUROC")
    axes[1].legend(fontsize=7)
    axes[1].grid(True, alpha=0.3)
    plt.suptitle(f"Optimizer Ablation Training Curves — {ds_name}", fontweight="bold")
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(
        os.path.join(working_dir, f"optimizer_ablation_curves_{ds_name}.png"),
        dpi=150,
        bbox_inches="tight",
    )
    plt.close()

# Cross-dataset bar chart
ds_names = [d[0] for d in datasets_config]
x = np.arange(len(ds_names))
width = 0.20
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(12, 6))
for i, opt_name in enumerate(OPTIMIZERS):
    vals = [results_auroc[opt_name][ds] for ds in ds_names]
    bars = ax.bar(
        x + i * width,
        vals,
        width,
        label=opt_name,
        color=opt_colors[opt_name],
        alpha=0.8,
        edgecolor="black",
    )
    for bar, v in zip(bars, vals):
        ax.text(
            bar.get_x() + bar.get_width() / 2,
            bar.get_height() + 0.005,
            f"{v:.3f}",
            ha="center",
            va="bottom",
            fontsize=7,
            fontweight="bold",
        )
ax.set_xlabel("Dataset")
ax.set_ylabel("Test AUROC")
ax.set_title("Optimizer Ablation — Cross-Dataset AUROC Comparison")
ax.set_xticks(x + width * 1.5)
ax.set_xticklabels(ds_names)
ax.legend()
ax.set_ylim(0, 1.1)
ax.axhline(y=0.5, color="red", linestyle="--", alpha=0.5, label="Random")
ax.grid(True, alpha=0.3, axis="y")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig(
    os.path.join(working_dir, "optimizer_ablation_cross_dataset.png"),
    dpi=150,
    bbox_inches="tight",
)
plt.close()

# Validation AUROC all datasets
fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, len(ds_names), figsize=(15, 5), sharey=False)
for ax, ds_name in zip(axes, ds_names):
    for opt_name in OPTIMIZERS:
        ax.plot(
            results_val_a[opt_name][ds_name],
            color=opt_colors[opt_name],
            label=opt_name,
            linewidth=1.5,
        )
    ax.set_title(ds_name)
    ax.set_xlabel("Epoch")
    ax.set_ylabel("Val AUROC")
    ax.legend(fontsize=8)
    ax.grid(True, alpha=0.3)
plt.suptitle("Validation AUROC by Optimizer (All Datasets)", fontweight="bold")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig(
    os.path.join(working_dir, "optimizer_ablation_val_auroc_all.png"),
    dpi=150,
    bbox_inches="tight",
)
plt.close()

# Per-optimizer convergence speed (final val AUROC vs epoch, one plot per optimizer)
fig, axes = plt.subplots(2, 2, figsize=(14, 10))
axes = axes.flatten()
for ax, opt_name in zip(axes, OPTIMIZERS):
    for ds_name in ds_names:
        ax.plot(results_val_a[opt_name][ds_name], label=ds_name, linewidth=1.5)
    ax.set_title(f"Optimizer: {opt_name}")
    ax.set_xlabel("Epoch")
    ax.set_ylabel("Val AUROC")
    ax.legend(fontsize=8)
    ax.grid(True, alpha=0.3)
plt.suptitle("Val AUROC per Optimizer Across Datasets", fontweight="bold")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig(
    os.path.join(working_dir, "optimizer_ablation_per_optimizer.png"),
    dpi=150,
    bbox_inches="tight",
)
plt.close()

np.save(os.path.join(working_dir, "experiment_data.npy"), experiment_data)
print("\nAll results saved to", working_dir)
print("Done!")
